key_insight: Droplet fusion and droplet shape recovery are both capillary-driven, but viscoelasticity makes them fundamentally different relaxation processes by introducing a competing stress-relaxation timescale.
engineering_relevance: For biomolecular condensates and soft matter design, purely viscous models can mispredict fusion kinetics and relaxation, so viscoelastic constitutive modeling is important for accurate dynamics.
method: Analytical small-deformation theory for shape recovery
Axisymmetric finite-element simulations using the Oldroyd-B model
Systematic comparison of Newtonian vs viscoelastic droplets under matched conditions
evidence_signal: The study evaluates theory and simulations against relaxation and fusion dynamics, including comparison with a stretched-exponential empirical formula, and reports clear deviations in viscoelastic regimes.
watch_item: Oldroyd-B and stretched-exponential fits may be inadequate when real condensates have more complex rheology, interfacial effects, or additional external-fluid dissipation.
paper_title: Droplet Fusion as a Relaxation Process: Comparison with Shape Recovery of Newtonian and Viscoelastic Droplets
